Act as a Senior Frontend Engineer and Senior Supply Chain & Operations Manager.
You are working in a React + TypeScript + Studio AI project for [PROJECT NAME, e.g. “Customs Broker Control”].

Goal

In addition to the main dashboard, you MUST create a standardized Weekly Report export feature.
This feature will be used by a central “Weekly Report” project to merge information from several dashboards (Broker, Logistics, Costs, Warehouse, etc.) and send a concise report to a Chinese Director who is very direct and numbers-oriented.

Requirements – Weekly Report Export

Add a button named exactly: Report - WK

Place it in a visible and fixed position (top right of the main header or filters area).

Style it as a primary action button.

When the user clicks Report - WK, generate and download a CSV file (UTF-8) with:

File name pattern:
WK_REPORT_[PROJECT_KEY]_[YYYY-MM-DD].csv
Example: WK_REPORT_BROKER_2025-12-09.csv

The CSV must contain a single header row with the following columns (in this order):

projectName
projectKey
weekRef
weekStart
weekEnd
generatedAt
ownerName
area
topKPI1_name
topKPI1_value
topKPI1_target
topKPI1_status
topKPI2_name
topKPI2_value
topKPI2_target
topKPI2_status
topKPI3_name
topKPI3_value
topKPI3_target
topKPI3_status
mainHighlights
mainIssuesRisks
operationalImpact
actionsNextWeek
dependenciesEscalations
overallStatus

Populate the CSV row automatically from the current state of the dashboard:

- projectName, projectKey, area are constants defined in the code.
- weekRef, weekStart, weekEnd are computed from the current date (consider Monday as the first day of the week).
- generatedAt is new Date().toISOString().
- topKPI*_name, topKPI*_value, topKPI*_target, topKPI*_status must be derived from the most important KPIs on this screen (e.g. clearance lead time, containers/day, demurrage cost, etc. depending on this specific project).
- mainHighlights, mainIssuesRisks, operationalImpact, actionsNextWeek, dependenciesEscalations should be auto-summaries based on the current numbers, trends and alerts.
- Keep the text very concise, English only.
- Use direct, executive tone because the reader is a Chinese director who likes clear, numeric information.
- overallStatus should be an automatic classification (Green / Yellow / Red) based on KPI variances vs target and presence of critical issues.

Implementation details (important)

Use pure TypeScript/React to generate the CSV (no external CSV libraries).

Implement a helper function to:
- Compute the current week (weekRef, weekStart, weekEnd).
- Build the CSV header and row as a string.
- Create a Blob with text/csv;charset=utf-8;.
- Trigger the download via a temporary <a> element and URL.createObjectURL.

The rest of the dashboard must continue working exactly as before.

Code style

- Use functional components and hooks.
- Extract the Weekly Report logic into small utilities if the file is getting large (e.g. buildWeeklyReportRow()).
- Add clear comments around the Report - WK button and the export function so it is easy to reuse in other projects.

Summary:
Keep the existing dashboard intact, and ADD a Report - WK button that generates a standard CSV weekly report with the columns listed above, filled automatically from the dashboard’s KPIs and status, in a concise executive style.

I've been examining existing code to find the ideal location for the "Report - WK" button, considering options like the `DashboardPage` or the `App` component's header. I'll highlight key performance areas, successes, and issues. I am also working on styling and adding the button within the `DashboardPage`. DI Channel Parametrization Aligned to DI Registration Date

Role:
Act as a Senior Data Engineer and Customs Operations Analyst designing a Brokerage KPI dashboard.

Core Rule (MANDATORY):
All DI Channel Parametrization (Green / Yellow / Red / Gray) must be attributed to the month of the DI Registration Date (Registro da DI) — not the month when the channel was parametrized by customs.

Business Context:

The dashboard shows Registered DIs filtered by Year / Month / Analyst / Cargo.

Customs channel parametrization (Verde, Amarelo, Vermelho, Cinza) may occur days or weeks after DI registration, sometimes in a different calendar month.

Critical Attribution Logic:

If a DI was registered in November, it must be counted in November KPIs,
even if:

Channel parametrization happened in December

Clearance (CI) happened in December

Physical inspection happened later

✔️ The DI “belongs” permanently to the month of its registration.

Data Rules to Enforce:

Primary Date Key:

Use DI Registration Date (Registro DI) as the sole reference for:

Monthly aggregation

KPI totals

Channel distribution (Green / Yellow / Red / Gray)

Channel Resolution:

For each registered DI:

Retrieve its final channel parametrization, regardless of when it occurred.

Attach that channel to the registration month.

Month Filter Behavior:

When filtering November 2025:

Include all DIs registered in November 2025

Display their actual channels, even if assigned in December 2025

Do NOT group channels by parametrization date.

Channel dates must never drive the month bucket.

Example (Must Be Correctly Reflected):

DI Number	DI Registration	Channel Date	Channel	KPI Month
DI-001	2025-11-28	2025-12-02	Green	Nov/2025
DI-002	2025-11-30	2025-12-05	Yellow	Nov/2025

✔️ Both channels must appear in November 2025 KPIs.

Dashboard Impact:

“Total DIs Registered” must match the base population.

“DI Channel Parametrization” must be a distribution of those same registered DIs.

No DI registered in November may disappear from November KPIs due to late parametrization.

Output Expectation:

Ensure logical consistency between:

Registered DI count

Channel distribution

Analyst productivity

Clearance KPIs

Any KPI derived from DI channels must be anchored to DI Registration Date, always.
